title: CCMH CRISIS LINE - 211info
text: COLUMBIA COMMUNITY MENTAL HEALTH CREEKSIDE CENTER CCMH CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF County crisis line providing support for people experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. Crisis therapists provide supportive counseling, outreach, assessment, and referral information to individuals experiencing an emotional crisis or interpersonal difficulties. Offers immediate, short-term help to individuals who experience an event that produces emotional, mental, physical, and behavioral distress or problems. 58646 McNulty Way Street Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 397-5211 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 397-5211 Weekdays Hotline (503) 782-4499 Anytime INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or walk in for crisis services FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Columbia County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Services: General Crisis Intervention Hotlines In Person Crisis Intervention Mental Health Crisis Lines Suicide Prevention Hotlines Other Services Offered At This Location: MENTAL HEALTH CARE FACILITIES - COLUMBIA COMMUNITY MENTAL HEALTH REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. COLUMBIA COMMUNITY MENTAL HEALTH CREEKSIDE CENTER
